mechanism_narrative: The first Federal Reserve rate hike after seven years near zero (FEDFUNDS crossing above 0.25%) removes policy uncertainty that had suppressed risk appetite. Because the hike is widely pre-announced and priced in, equities rally on resolution of uncertainty rather than sell off on tightening fears, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 0.25% for the first time since 2008
regime_hint: policy liftoff from zero lower bound
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of instances following a first-hike liftoff event across historical liftoff episodes, or the mean post-liftoff 5-day return is not significantly different from the unconditional 5-day mean (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A persistent multi-month downtrend in UNRATE reflects tightening labor markets and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings expectations. This translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month, independent of any specific threshold.
failure_condition: Rank IC between UNRATE 3-month change and forward 20-day SPX return is below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or the sign relationship reverses in more than one of the last five expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in month-over-month RSAFS retail sales growth above trend signals stronger-than-expected consumer demand heading into the holiday season, prompting upward revisions to earnings forecasts and positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth exceeds 0.5% for two consecutive releases
failure_condition: In more than 50% of historical instances where RSAFS MoM growth exceeds 0.5% twice consecutively, the subsequent 20-day SPX return is negative, or Rank IC falls below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained year-over-year decline in INDPRO signals contraction in the manufacturing sector, foreshadowing weaker corporate earnings and broader economic slowdown, which drives negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO year-over-year growth turns negative for two consecutive months
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances following two consecutive months of negative INDPRO YoY growth, or effect is not significant at p < 0.10 across rolling out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is rising simultaneously with UNRATE falling below a structural threshold, the joint signal indicates an overheating economy prompting the Fed toward a faster tightening path than markets expect, increasing discount-rate risk and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month. Neither series alone captures this overheating dynamic; it requires their joint co-movement.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises for 2 consecutive months while UNRATE is below 5.2%
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tightening labor market
failure_condition: The joint FEDFUNDS-rising/UNRATE-low trigger produces positive mean 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of historical occurrences, or the interaction term's marginal predictive power (over either series alone) is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising HOUST housing starts signal strengthening residential construction and forward-looking consumer confidence. This relationship is positive for S&P 500 returns during early-to-mid expansion regimes but reverses or weakens once the cycle matures and overbuilding risk emerges, so the sign of the effect depends on which phase of the cycle is active.
failure_condition: The positive HOUST-to-forward-return relationship fails to hold (Rank IC < 0.03 or sign reversal) when tested specifically within early-to-mid expansion regime windows identified by NBER cycle dating, over any rolling 3-year subsample.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS retail sales growth and INDPRO industrial production growth are both accelerating simultaneously, this joint confirmation of demand-side and supply-side strength signals broad-based economic momentum that is more reliable than either indicator alone, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM growth > 0.3% and INDPRO MoM growth > 0.2% in the same release month
failure_condition: Joint-trigger instances produce SPX 20-day forward returns statistically indistinguishable from unconditional baseline (p > 0.10), or the interaction adds no incremental Rank IC over either single-series signal alone across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A persistently low and stable CPIAUCSL inflation trend, well below the Fed's 2% target, keeps expected future policy tightening gradual, supporting sustained equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter regardless of short-term fluctuations.
failure_condition: The relationship between low year-over-year CPIAUCSL growth (below 2%) and positive 60-day forward SPX returns fails to hold with Rank IC below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or reverses sign during any low-inflation subperiod.
